dailyJudgementSnippet: OpenAI won a quiet day by getting the cleanest brand-specific comparison: Codex was described as already able to work directly with common APIs and docs workflows, while most other branded discussion centered on pricing or tooling friction [E1].
winnerExplanation: OpenAI finished on top because its only meaningful signal was favorable and concrete: in a Google tooling thread, a commenter contrasted Codex's built-in practical capability with a more cumbersome MCP-based setup [E1]. Coverage was light, but that still beat a day where Anthropic's clearest branded thread was dominated by fast-mode pricing complaints [E2] and Google Gemini's main discussion drew skepticism about complexity, token appetite, and stale answers [E3].

openai.judgementSnippet: Codex surfaced mainly as a favorable comparison point: a commenter in Google's docs/MCP thread said Codex already handles common API-and-doc workflows with much less setup, which gave OpenAI the day's clearest practical endorsement [E1].

anthropic.judgementSnippet: Claude Code's fast mode launch drew heavy pushback on price and billing, with commenters calling out "2.5x the speed at 6x the price," forced extra-usage charges, and general fatigue with another Claude launch; small acknowledgements of Claude Code's strength did not change the thread's tone [E2].

google_gemini.judgementSnippet: Google's Developer Knowledge API/MCP launch was met with "why is this so complicated?" reactions, plus complaints that Gemini CLI burns too many tokens and misses current platform details; a separate Claude-thread comparison helped on speed/price, but that same comment still said Gemini lagged on coding and agentic strength [E3][E4].

evidence.summary: In the Google Developer Knowledge API thread, a commenter said Codex already knows how to work with common services and APIs like Jira, Confluence, GitLab, Prometheus, and SQL with minimal setup, making OpenAI the favorable comparison point.

The Claude Code fast mode discussion focused on price and billing complaints: commenters highlighted "2.5x the speed at 6x the price," noted that fast mode always bills as extra usage, and one joked HN should just CNAME to the Claude marketing blog.

Google's Developer Knowledge API/MCP announcement drew skeptical comments asking why the docs workflow was so complicated, with added complaints that Gemini CLI has an "insane appetite for tokens" and weak knowledge of current platform details.

In Anthropic's fast mode thread, Gemini 3 Pro was praised for cheaper, faster token throughput, but the same comment said it was worse at coding and Gemini CLI lacked Claude Code's agentic strength, leaving a mixed signal rather than a clear win.
